mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as discount rates rise and growth expectations fall, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following high inflation prints during tightening periods, or the 5-day forward return correlation with CPI surprises turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate weakening residential investment and reduced construction activity, which drags on aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Housing starts declines coincide with positive equity returns in >70% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply chain conditions and higher factory utilization, which boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict short-term equity gains, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain congestion.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k while UNRATE falls
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates surpassing 3.5 percent indicate restrictive monetary policy that suppresses borrowing and investment, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets sustain positive returns when rates remain above 3.5%, or the negative predictive relationship breaks down during periods of strong GDP growth.
